description_long: This object is a monument featuring a sculpted bird, likely an eagle, perched atop a rough-hewn rectangular stone pedestal. The bird is rendered with outstretched wings, detailed feathers, and a prominent hooked beak, standing on a spherical element. The pedestal is made of reddish-brown stone with a rugged, natural texture and has a metal plaque affixed to its front, inscribed with commemorative text in Polish. The bird sculpture appears metallic, contrasting with the stone base, and is mounted on a small circular plinth. The overall form is solid and imposing, designed to serve as a public memorial or marker. The monument's materials and construction suggest durability and permanence in an outdoor urban setting.
description: A commemorative monument with a metallic eagle sculpture atop a rugged stone pedestal, featuring a front-facing inscribed plaque.
description_view_0: Front view showing the eagle with wings spread and the commemorative plaque clearly visible on the stone pedestal.
description_view_1: Angled front-left view highlighting the eagle's profile and the plaque's placement on the textured stone.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the eagle with outstretched wings and the unadorned stone surface.
description_view_3: Angled rear-right view showing the eagle's side and the rough stone without the plaque.
